name: navigation-menu
description: A site navigation landmark (<nav>, not a menu role) with real <a href> links and optional rich dropdown panels revealed on hover/focus-within, CSS-only (Radix NavigationMenu model). Server-first PARTIAL (ADR-0012, Wave 2): active-state comes from the server-rendered route via aria-current, the panel reveal is pure CSS, so there is no client state and no wire component. Replaces the <lv-navigation-menu> Lit island. Plain HTML + Tailwind + --lv-* tokens, zero JS, CSP-clean.
docs: Copies the navigation-menu family to your JTE root (lievit/navigation-menu.jte + lievit/navigation-menu/{link,menu}.jte), reached as @template.lievit.navigation-menu(...). Compose the <li> items in the `content` slot from @template.lievit.navigation-menu.menu (a trigger + rich panel) and @template.lievit.navigation-menu.link (a bare link). Derive each link's `active` from the route you are rendering (the controller knows the current page); the partial sets aria-current="page" on it. The panel reveals on CSS hover/focus-within, no JS. The source is yours: edit freely.
